AI-OS ← Back to setup
Field Guide

Get the most from every command.

Each command explained in plain language up top, with the depth power users want below — what it does, when to reach for it, a walkthrough, and the advanced moves. New to this? The setup walkthrough gets you running in about five minutes; come back here when you want to go deeper.

{{GUIDE_INDEX}}

Every command in your core toolkit is covered below. Start with the ones you'll reach for daily — /brief, /triage, /prep — and dip into the rest as you need them. The second-act commands — /career, /projects, /learning — and the scheduler are documented where you hire them: the second act and scheduler sections of the setup page.

Start here

A day with your AI OS.

Don't worry about learning commands — learn the moments. Here's how a normal day maps onto your toolkit; the full reference for each command is below.

It's 8:40am. Three meetings ahead and you're not sure what's waiting on you.
/brief

Your whole day on one screen — today's meetings and who's in them, what needs a reply, top priorities, deadlines this week — before your first coffee.

Monday morning, and the weekend piled up sixty unread emails.
/triage

One pass sorts your inbox into Needs Reply / FYI / Noise and flags calendar conflicts. Nothing is sent — just sorted, so you open a clear inbox.

A call in ten minutes and you can't remember where it last landed.
/prep

The invite, the mail thread, the relevant project and people notes, the last decisions and open items — plus the questions likely to come up. You walk in ready.

It's Friday and your status update is due — again.
/weekly

Your weekly review is already drafted — what shipped, what slipped or is blocked, decisions, next week's shape — with a status update ready to edit and send. (Just one project? /status.)

"Wait — what did we actually decide about the redesign?"
/recall

One search across everything you know — your memory and your notes — answered with citations and honest about any gaps. (Want just the deep dive into your notes? /wiki.)

It drafted something in the wrong tone, and you fixed it.
/improve

Correct it once, run /improve, and the lesson becomes a durable rule — the system gets a little more "you" every time you steer it.

Other moments, and the command they call for:

A real decision to weigh — a hire, a vendor, a strategy call/board
A pile of reading, clippings, or meeting transcripts to make sense of/ingest
Learning a topic over many sessions, without losing your place/teach
Explaining a process or system to someone (or yourself)/workflow-visualizer
Protecting it all against a lost or wiped laptop — and bringing it back/backup  ·  /restore
Checking everything's still healthy — connectors, memory, backups/doctor
Keeping your memory lean and true as it grows, or building a new command/memory-prune  ·  /skill-center

New here? The setup walkthrough gets you running in about five minutes. The full reference for every command is just below.

{{GUIDE_SECTIONS}}
Part of your AI-OS. Everything here runs locally, reads before it writes, and sends nothing without your OK. · Back to setup →
